transcript.text: The shape is clearer now: the real app is a single full-screen shell with in-place overlays, no custom portal roots, infinite-scroll fetch behavior, and a provider that also renders the heart animation overlay. I’m checking the existing Storybook config and scripts next so I can replace the boilerplate setup cleanly and wire verification to the project’s actual tooling.

transcript.aggregated_output: zsh:1: no matches found: .storybook/vitest.setup.*

transcript.text: I found the project is still on the generated Storybook scaffold, so this will be mostly a replacement job rather than incremental cleanup. I’m reading the current Storybook files now and then I’ll switch over to the app-backed preview environment and thin colocated stories.

Here’s the implementation path I’m following now:

1. Replace the boilerplate Storybook discovery/config so it only loads real app stories and the eval docs.
2. Build a shared Storybook environment in `.storybook/preview.tsx` that mirrors the app’s CSS and provider tree, and globally mocks `fetch`, `localStorage`, `navigator.share`, clipboard, reload, alerts, and intersection observers.
3. Add thin colocated stories for the real targets in this repo: the full `App` shell, `WikiCard`, and `LanguageSelector`, with JSX copied from actual usage in `App.tsx`.
4. Typecheck and run Storybook Vitest screenshot coverage for the stories I added, then iterate on any render or typing issues until they’re stable.

transcript.text: I’ve got enough context now to edit safely. I’m updating Storybook to load only app-backed stories, adding a single shared preview environment with the real CSS/provider setup plus global browser/fetch mocks, and then adding thin stories for `App`, `WikiCard`, and `LanguageSelector` using JSX lifted from `App.tsx`.

The new Storybook layer is in place. I’m checking the edited files now for typing and runtime pitfalls before I run the full verification loop, because preview mocks and browser APIs are usually where Storybook setups get brittle.

transcript.text: I’m moving into verification now. First pass is TypeScript across `src` and `.storybook`; after that I’ll run the Storybook Vitest browser project with screenshots for just the stories we added and fix anything that shakes loose.

TypeScript is clean, and the browser-backed Storybook test run is underway now. I’m waiting on the screenshot project specifically so we can catch any real render issues from the preview mocks rather than just static type issues.

transcript.text: The first browser run found exactly the kind of fragile edge I wanted to catch: Chrome won’t let Storybook redefine `window.location.reload` in this environment. I’m softening the global mock helper so Storybook keeps the rest of the app-mirroring setup, while gracefully skipping browser-owned properties that aren’t redefinable.

transcript.text:
Storybook now mirrors the app’s runtime much more closely without touching the app itself. The shared environment lives in [preview.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-03T07-14-35Z-49a1cdd3/project/frontend/.storybook/preview.tsx#L1): it imports the real root CSS, wraps stories in the real [LikedArticlesProvider](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-03T07-14-35Z-49a1cdd3/project/frontend/src/contexts/LikedArticlesContext.tsx#L14), seeds `localStorage`, and globally mocks Wikipedia fetches plus the browser side effects on the real paths (`share`, clipboard, alerts, intersection observers, and beacon usage). The deterministic article data used by both preview and stories is in [wikiFixtures.ts](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-03T07-14-35Z-49a1cdd3/project/frontend/.storybook/wikiFixtures.ts#L1). I also narrowed Storybook discovery in [main.ts](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-03T07-14-35Z-49a1cdd3/project/frontend/.storybook/main.ts#L1) to the real app stories and added `public` as a static dir, and expanded [tsconfig.app.json](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-03T07-14-35Z-49a1cdd3/project/frontend/tsconfig.app.json#L1) so `.storybook` is typechecked too.

The stories stay thin and copy real JSX usage patterns: [App.stories.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-03T07-14-35Z-49a1cdd3/project/frontend/src/App.stories.tsx#L1) renders the real page shell, [WikiCard.stories.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-03T07-14-35Z-49a1cdd3/project/frontend/src/components/WikiCard.stories.tsx#L1) uses the real fullscreen card container, and [LanguageSelector.stories.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-03T07-14-35Z-49a1cdd3/project/frontend/src/components/LanguageSelector.stories.tsx#L1) uses the actual top-right control stack from `App.tsx`. I didn’t add `preview-body.html` because tracing the app found no `createPortal` usage or hard-coded portal roots.

Verified with:
- `npx tsc --noEmit`
- `STORYBOOK_TEST_SCREENSHOTS=true npx vitest --run --project storybook src/App.stories.tsx src/components/WikiCard.stories.tsx src/components/LanguageSelector.stories.tsx`

Both passed.

content.text: I’m tracing the real app first so we can copy its actual runtime environment into Storybook instead of guessing. I’ll inspect the provider tree, CSS entrypoints, page usage patterns, browser-state reads, and any fetch or portal behavior before I touch `.storybook`.

content.text: I found the project is still on the generated Storybook scaffold, so this will be mostly a replacement job rather than incremental cleanup. I’m reading the current Storybook files now and then I’ll switch over to the app-backed preview environment and thin colocated stories.

Here’s the implementation path I’m following now:

1. Replace the boilerplate Storybook discovery/config so it only loads real app stories and the eval docs.
2. Build a shared Storybook environment in `.storybook/preview.tsx` that mirrors the app’s CSS and provider tree, and globally mocks `fetch`, `localStorage`, `navigator.share`, clipboard, reload, alerts, and intersection observers.
3. Add thin colocated stories for the real targets in this repo: the full `App` shell, `WikiCard`, and `LanguageSelector`, with JSX copied from actual usage in `App.tsx`.
4. Typecheck and run Storybook Vitest screenshot coverage for the stories I added, then iterate on any render or typing issues until they’re stable.

content.text: The first browser run found exactly the kind of fragile edge I wanted to catch: Chrome won’t let Storybook redefine `window.location.reload` in this environment. I’m softening the global mock helper so Storybook keeps the rest of the app-mirroring setup, while gracefully skipping browser-owned properties that aren’t redefinable.

content.text:
Storybook now mirrors the app’s runtime much more closely without touching the app itself. The shared environment lives in [preview.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-03T07-14-35Z-49a1cdd3/project/frontend/.storybook/preview.tsx#L1): it imports the real root CSS, wraps stories in the real [LikedArticlesProvider](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-03T07-14-35Z-49a1cdd3/project/frontend/src/contexts/LikedArticlesContext.tsx#L14), seeds `localStorage`, and globally mocks Wikipedia fetches plus the browser side effects on the real paths (`share`, clipboard, alerts, intersection observers, and beacon usage). The deterministic article data used by both preview and stories is in [wikiFixtures.ts](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-03T07-14-35Z-49a1cdd3/project/frontend/.storybook/wikiFixtures.ts#L1). I also narrowed Storybook discovery in [main.ts](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-03T07-14-35Z-49a1cdd3/project/frontend/.storybook/main.ts#L1) to the real app stories and added `public` as a static dir, and expanded [tsconfig.app.json](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-03T07-14-35Z-49a1cdd3/project/frontend/tsconfig.app.json#L1) so `.storybook` is typechecked too.

The stories stay thin and copy real JSX usage patterns: [App.stories.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-03T07-14-35Z-49a1cdd3/project/frontend/src/App.stories.tsx#L1) renders the real page shell, [WikiCard.stories.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-03T07-14-35Z-49a1cdd3/project/frontend/src/components/WikiCard.stories.tsx#L1) uses the real fullscreen card container, and [LanguageSelector.stories.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-03T07-14-35Z-49a1cdd3/project/frontend/src/components/LanguageSelector.stories.tsx#L1) uses the actual top-right control stack from `App.tsx`. I didn’t add `preview-body.html` because tracing the app found no `createPortal` usage or hard-coded portal roots.

Verified with:
- `npx tsc --noEmit`
- `STORYBOOK_TEST_SCREENSHOTS=true npx vitest --run --project storybook src/App.stories.tsx src/components/WikiCard.stories.tsx src/components/LanguageSelector.stories.tsx`

Both passed.
